title: oenanthe pumila
intro: Oenanthe pumila, also known as dwarf water parsley, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Oenanthe pumila include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
